title: 48-hour integration accounts retain at 2.3x the 90-day rate
anchor: 26 of 47 Q1 implementations connected their first integration within 48 hours of kickoff.
connect: Accounts that waited past the first week to integrate retained at standard rates.
body: 26 of 47 Q1 implementations connected their first integration within 48 hours of kickoff. Accounts that waited past the first week to integrate retained at standard rates.
trace: The 48-hour integration window appears to be a leading indicator of long-term account success, with a clear retention advantage over delayed integrations.

title: Tier-2 activation moves to 78% inside 14 days
anchor: 78% of Tier-2 implementations reached first value inside the two-week window this quarter.
connect: Q4 Tier-2 activation sat at 54% over the same timeframe.
body: 78% of Tier-2 implementations reached first value inside the two-week window this quarter. Q4 Tier-2 activation sat at 54% over the same timeframe.
trace: Significant improvement in mid-complexity tier activation rate, suggesting process or tooling improvements are taking hold.

title: Mid-market implementations clear go-live 6 days ahead of plan
anchor: 22 completed Q1 mid-market implementations averaged Day 45 go-live versus the Day 51 project plan.
connect: This is the first quarter mid-market has consistently beaten the implementation timeline.
body: 22 completed Q1 mid-market implementations averaged Day 45 go-live versus the Day 51 project plan. This is the first quarter mid-market has consistently beaten the implementation timeline.
trace: Mid-market implementations are running more efficiently than planned, indicating improved process execution or more accurate scoping.

title: Configuration template cuts enterprise sign-off time by one week
anchor: Enterprise configuration sign-off moved from Day 18 to Day 11 after the March 1 template launch.
connect: 14 enterprise implementations in Q1 used the pre-kickoff configuration approach.
body: Enterprise configuration sign-off moved from Day 18 to Day 11 after the March 1 template launch. 14 enterprise implementations in Q1 used the pre-kickoff configuration approach.
trace: The pre-kickoff configuration template is compressing the enterprise sign-off cycle by approximately 7 days, improving overall delivery speed.

title: Healthcare vertical runs fastest segment median at 38 days
anchor: 11 healthcare go-lives this quarter completed in 38 days median kickoff-to-go-live.
connect: This is the fastest segment median across the entire implementation portfolio.
body: 11 healthcare go-lives this quarter completed in 38 days median kickoff-to-go-live. This is the fastest segment median across the entire implementation portfolio.
trace: Healthcare vertical consistently delivers the fastest implementations despite typically complex data requirements.

title: Data integration milestone completion reaches 71% inside 10 days
anchor: 71% of 38 active implementations cleared the integration milestone within 10 days of kickoff this quarter.
connect: Q4 integration milestone completion sat at 58% over the same window.
body: 71% of 38 active implementations cleared the integration milestone within 10 days of kickoff this quarter. Q4 integration milestone completion sat at 58% over the same window.
trace: Integration milestone completion is improving, suggesting better stakeholder preparation or technical process refinement.

title: Multi-user activation by Day 30 drives 3x retention strength
anchor: 31 of 47 Q1 implementations crossed the 3-user threshold by Day 30.
connect: Single-user activations showed standard 90-day retention patterns across the same cohort.
body: 31 of 47 Q1 implementations crossed the 3-user threshold by Day 30. Single-user activations showed standard 90-day retention patterns across the same cohort.
trace: Breadth of user activation within the first month appears to be a strong predictor of account retention success.

title: Onboarding checklist completion rises to 34% after template launch
anchor: Checklist completion moved from 22% to 34% after the March 1 configuration template launched.
connect: The UserGuiding industry baseline for B2B SaaS onboarding sits at 19.2%.
body: Checklist completion moved from 22% to 34% after the March 1 configuration template launched. The UserGuiding industry baseline for B2B SaaS onboarding sits at 19.2%.
trace: The configuration template is improving customer engagement with the onboarding process, driving above-industry completion rates.

title: Kickoff stakeholder coverage reaches 64% with 3+ named participants
anchor: 64% of 51 kickoffs this quarter listed 3 or more named stakeholders on the project plan.
connect: Prior quarter stakeholder coverage sat at 39% across kickoffs.
body: 64% of 51 kickoffs this quarter listed 3 or more named stakeholders on the project plan. Prior quarter stakeholder coverage sat at 39% across kickoffs.
trace: Stakeholder identification and engagement at project kickoff has improved significantly, suggesting better pre-implementation discovery.

title: Self-serve sandbox lifts Tier-1 activation 19 percentage points
anchor: Tier-1 implementations reached first value inside 14 days at 81% after the March 12 sandbox launch.
connect: Pre-launch Tier-1 activation sat at 62% across 36 accounts.
body: Tier-1 implementations reached first value inside 14 days at 81% after the March 12 sandbox launch. Pre-launch Tier-1 activation sat at 62% across 36 accounts.
trace: The self-serve sandbox is enabling customers to explore and configure independently before formal kickoff, accelerating simple-tier implementations.

title: Complete use-case capture shortens go-live by 12 days
anchor: 34 Q1 implementations with complete pre-close documentation reached go-live 12 days earlier than incomplete accounts.
connect: This represents the strongest correlation between sales handoff quality and implementation velocity on record.
body: 34 Q1 implementations with complete pre-close documentation reached go-live 12 days earlier than incomplete accounts. This represents the strongest correlation between sales handoff quality and implementation velocity on record.
trace: Sales-to-implementation handoff quality appears to be a major driver of implementation timeline efficiency.

title: Mid-market implementation NPS jumps 18 points to 59
anchor: Implementation NPS moved from 41 to 59 across 28 surveyed mid-market go-lives this quarter.
connect: This is the largest segment-level NPS improvement in implementation history.
body: Implementation NPS moved from 41 to 59 across 28 surveyed mid-market go-lives this quarter. This is the largest segment-level NPS improvement in implementation history.
trace: Mid-market customer satisfaction with the implementation process has improved dramatically, suggesting process refinements are working.

title: Product-blocker resolution tightens from 9 days to 4 days
anchor: 17 implementation-tagged Engineering issues resolved in a 4-day median this quarter.
connect: Prior quarter product-blocker resolution ran at 9 days median.
body: 17 implementation-tagged Engineering issues resolved in a 4-day median this quarter. Prior quarter product-blocker resolution ran at 9 days median.
trace: Engineering response to implementation-specific product issues has accelerated significantly, reducing customer wait times.

title: Enterprise handoff brief acknowledgment reaches 92% inside 48 hours
anchor: 92% of 26 enterprise go-lives had the structured handoff brief acknowledged by the receiving CSM within 48 hours.
connect: This represents near-complete CSM engagement with the implementation-to-ongoing handoff process.
body: 92% of 26 enterprise go-lives had the structured handoff brief acknowledged by the receiving CSM within 48 hours. This represents near-complete CSM engagement with the implementation-to-ongoing handoff process.
trace: The structured handoff process is working effectively, ensuring smooth transition from implementation to ongoing customer success management.

title: Implementation support response compresses to 2.4 hours median
anchor: 142 implementation-tagged support tickets received first response in 2.4 hours median this quarter.
connect: Pre-queue first response sat at 6 hours median before the dedicated implementation support queue launched.
body: 142 implementation-tagged support tickets received first response in 2.4 hours median this quarter. Pre-queue first response sat at 6 hours median before the dedicated implementation support queue launched.
trace: The dedicated implementation support queue is significantly reducing customer wait times for implementation-specific issues and questions.

title: Template launch correlates with three velocity improvements
anchor: March 1 configuration template launch preceded improvements in enterprise sign-off, checklist completion, and Tier-1 activation.
connect: Enterprise sign-off compressed 7 days, checklist completion rose 12 points, and Tier-1 activation gained 19 points.
body: March 1 configuration template launch preceded improvements in enterprise sign-off, checklist completion, and Tier-1 activation. Enterprise sign-off compressed 7 days, checklist completion rose 12 points, and Tier-1 activation gained 19 points.
trace: The configuration template appears to be driving improvements across multiple complexity tiers and process stages simultaneously.

title: Q1 implementation velocity patterns show consistent acceleration
anchor: Mid-market beat plan by 6 days, enterprise configuration compressed by 7 days, and healthcare segment ran fastest median.
connect: Integration milestone completion and support response times both improved over the same quarter.
body: Mid-market beat plan by 6 days, enterprise configuration compressed by 7 days, and healthcare segment ran fastest median. Integration milestone completion and support response times both improved over the same quarter.
trace: Multiple velocity metrics are improving simultaneously across different segments and process stages, suggesting systematic implementation process enhancement.

title: Early activation windows predict long-term account retention
anchor: 48-hour integration and Day 30 multi-user activation both correlate with retention multiples above 2x.
connect: These early-implementation milestones appear to be leading indicators of account success at 90 days.
body: 48-hour integration and Day 30 multi-user activation both correlate with retention multiples above 2x. These early-implementation milestones appear to be leading indicators of account success at 90 days.
trace: Two distinct early-implementation milestones are showing similar predictive power for long-term account retention success.

title: Cross-functional handoff quality drives implementation timeline variance
anchor: Complete sales handoffs save 12 days, while 92% of CSM handoff briefs receive 48-hour acknowledgment.
connect: Both upstream and downstream handoff quality appear to influence overall implementation efficiency.
body: Complete sales handoffs save 12 days, while 92% of CSM handoff briefs receive 48-hour acknowledgment. Both upstream and downstream handoff quality appear to influence overall implementation efficiency.
trace: Implementation success appears to depend significantly on the quality of both inbound handoffs from Sales and outbound handoffs to Customer Success.

title: Support infrastructure improvements cluster in Q1
anchor: Product-blocker resolution accelerated from 9 to 4 days while support response compressed from 6 to 2.4 hours.
connect: The dedicated implementation support queue launched during the same period as these velocity improvements.
body: Product-blocker resolution accelerated from 9 to 4 days while support response compressed from 6 to 2.4 hours. The dedicated implementation support queue launched during the same period as these velocity improvements.
trace: Both Engineering and Support response times to implementation issues improved simultaneously, suggesting coordinated infrastructure enhancements.
